description:
# balls — greenfield architecture (working document)

Updated as topics close. Sibling topic tasks track open questions.

## §0 Overview

balls is a task tracker. State lives entirely on one branch (default `balls`) of a git repo. All persistence is git, local-first. The `tracker` plugin (shipped with balls) is the only thing that talks to a remote. Other plugins are user-supplied binaries invoked in sequence on every state change. Federation is a tracker config: point at someone else's balls branch and you shadow theirs underneath yours.

Base balls is the smallest possible thing: it knows how to find/create a local git repo at `operating/` and commit to its `balls` branch. Everything that connects to the world is a plugin. Stealth mode is simply "no tracker plugin" — base balls falls back to its own local repo.

## §1 XDG layout

```
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/balls/
  config.yaml                                      # user-level config

$XDG_STATE_HOME/balls/
  plugins/                                         # each plugin owns one subtree
    tracker/<url-encoded-remote>/                  # clone of the balls branch
      .git/  tasks/  config/
      change-attempts/<uuid>/                      # in-flight worktrees
    <other>/<plugin-territory>/

  clones/<url-encoded-local-path>/                 # per-invocation-path binding
    binding.yaml                                   # which tracker remote (if any)
    operating/                                     # the git repo balls operates against
                                                   # tracker enabled: symlink → plugins/tracker/<remote>/
                                                   # tracker disabled: a real dir
    logs/<plugin>/plugin.log                       # suggested; plugins do as they please
```

- URLs and paths are percent-encoded (no hashing) so dir names stay inspectable.
- Tracker owns its own clone; it does NOT share refs with the user's working repo. Two clones with the same remote URL share one tracker dir.
- Change-attempt worktrees are git worktrees of the tracker clone.

## §2 Branch layout (committed to `balls`)

```
tasks/<ball-id>.md                # one file per task
config/balls.yaml                 # federated balls config
config/plugins/<name>/...         # each plugin's federated config; balls never reads it
```

No archive dir. Closed/dropped balls delete their file — history lives in `git log`.

## §3 Ball schema

`tasks/<id>.md`:

```markdown
---
id: bl-1234
title: Refactor the foo system
state: open
created: 2026-05-27T14:32:00Z
updated: 2026-05-28T09:15:00Z
claimant: orionriver@gmail.com
parent: bl-1000
deps: [bl-1100, bl-1200]
tags: [refactor, infra]
---

# Description

Free-form markdown.
```

- `id`: string-safe; default scheme `bl-XXXX`; configurable.
- `state`: `open | claimed | review`. Extensible via config (§see states-as-config topic).
- `created` / `updated`: RFC3339 UTC.
- `claimant`, `parent`, `deps`, `tags`: optional.
- Unknown frontmatter keys are preserved on writeback.
- Terminal states (`close`, `drop`) DELETE the file; recovery is via `git log` on the balls branch.

## §4 Config schema

**Layers, innermost wins:**
1. CLI flags
2. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/balls/config.yaml`
3. `config/balls.yaml` on the satellite balls branch
4. `config/balls.yaml` on the center balls branch (only if `config/plugins/tracker/remote.yaml` points at one; one hop)
5. Built-in defaults

**Merge semantics:**
- Scalar/object fields: innermost layer fully replaces outer.
- List fields: bare `<field>` = full replacement. Compose with `<field>_prepend`, `<field>_append`, `<field>_ban`. No `_override` (bare assignment already replaces).

**Built-in fields (initial set):**

- `branch` (string, default `"balls"`)
- `id_scheme` (object, default `{ prefix: "bl-", length: 4 }`)
- `plugins` (list of `{name, path}`, default `[{name: tracker, path: balls-tracker}]`)
- `default_state` (string, default `"open"`)
- `states` (list, default `[open, claimed, review]`) — terminals (`close`, `drop`) are ops, not states

## §5 Commit message protocol

Every change-attempt commit:

```
<subject — free-form, human-readable>

op: close
ball: bl-1234
from-state: claimed
to-state: closed
actor: orionriver@gmail.com

<free-form body>
```

Sections are blank-line separated. The k:v block is parsed by balls and forwarded to plugins in the payload. Subject and body are display.

**Reserved keys** (balls writes these): `op`, `ball`, `actor`, `from-state`, `to-state`, `protocol`. Plugins MAY add their own.

## §6 Plugin contract

A plugin is a single binary. balls invokes it with a verb. Internal dispatcher: `bl plugin <name> <verb>` is the canonical entrypoint; balls dogfoods it.

```
<bin> protocol
  → stdout (JSON): { version: 1, verbs: ["pre-invoke","invoke","rollback","init","sync"] }
  → exit: 0

<bin> <verb>
  cwd:       change-attempt worktree (invoke/rollback) or operating/ (init/sync/prime)
  env:       BALLS_PROTOCOL=1, BALLS_PLUGIN_NAME=<name>  (CLI flags can override)
  stdin:     payload (JSON or YAML)
  stdout:    structured response (pre-invoke: field_overrides + metadata_additions; others: diagnostics)
  stderr:    human log; streamed and captured to clones/<path>/logs/<name>/
  exit:      0 = ok; non-zero = abort + rollback prior plugins in reverse
```

balls only calls verbs the plugin declares in `protocol` output.

## §7 Wire payloads

**Philosophy:** plugins get meaning directly on the wire — content + intent — not commit hashes to reverse-engineer. Hashes appear when relevant (tracker uses them) but are reference, not load-bearing.

**pre-invoke payload (stdin):**

```yaml
protocol: 1
verb: pre-invoke
plugin_name: jira
actor: orionriver@gmail.com
binding: { remote: ..., branch: ..., operating: ... }

command:
  op: create               # create | claim | unclaim | review | close | drop | update | config
  ball_id: null            # null on create; plugin MAY assign
  field_changes:           # what balls intends to write
    title: "Refactor foo"
    state: open
  body_change: "..."

current_state: null        # null on create; { frontmatter, body } otherwise
head_commit: 4f3a9c...     # reference only
```

**pre-invoke return channel (stdout JSON):**

```yaml
field_overrides:           # merged into command.field_changes
  id: "PROJ-1234"
metadata_additions:        # appended to commit message k:v block
  jira-id: PROJ-1234
```

Empty stdout = accept as-is. Non-zero exit = reject. balls applies overrides between plugin calls so the next plugin sees prior contributions.

**invoke payload (post-write):**

```yaml
protocol: 1
verb: invoke
plugin_name: tracker
commit: 9bf21e...
previous_commit: 4f3a9c...

command: { ... }           # final form, post pre-chain mutations
metadata: { ... }          # parsed from commit message k:v block

previous_state:            # ball at HEAD^; null for create
  frontmatter: {...}
  body: "..."
current_state:             # ball at HEAD; null for close/drop
  frontmatter: {...}
  body: "..."
```

**rollback payload:** same shape as the verb being undone, plus `rolling_back: invoke|pre-invoke`. Plugin tracks its own intermediate state.

## §8 Op lifecycle (state-change ops)

```
1. Snapshot plugin list.
2. Create change-attempt worktree, apply file changes (no commit yet).
3. For each plugin: call pre-invoke. Non-zero → rollback prior pre-invokes in reverse + abort.
4. Apply field_overrides from pre-invoke responses to the proposed change.
5. Commit with structured message.
6. For each plugin: call invoke. Non-zero → rollback prior invokes AND prior pre-invokes in reverse + abort.
7. Merge --no-ff into operating/. Done.
```

Reads (`bl list`, `bl show`) never invoke plugins.

## §9 bl init lifecycle

```
1. Resolve handle (local_path, enc_local, clone_dir).
2. Guard re-init (--reinit override).
3. Resolve initial plugin list (CLI > XDG > defaults; no federation yet).
4. Resolve tracker remote (only if tracker on chain). --remote > XDG > git remote get-url origin. If none and tracker is on the chain: hard fail.
5. Materialize clone_dir; write binding.yaml.
6. Run the plugin chain with verb=init. Tracker: clone the balls branch into plugins/tracker/<enc>/ (or scaffold orphan), symlink operating/ to it. Other plugins: their territory.
7. Look at operating/. Valid git repo on `balls` branch? Yes → done. No → git init it, checkout -b balls, seed config + empty tasks/, commit.
8. Reread config from federated layers, refresh binding.yaml if plugin list changed.
```

Step 7 is the converging predicate: tracker-symlinked, re-init, or fresh stealth all look identical to base balls.

## Open topics (see sibling tasks)

Each topic, when settled, becomes a new §here.

- Plugin dispatcher contract (protocol verb, recursion, install + snapshot)
- Commit message k:v parser rules
- Pre-invoke return channel + field-conflict policy
- bl new walkthrough (create flow, plugin-assigned id)
- State transitions (claim/unclaim/review) walkthrough
- Terminal ops (close/drop) walkthrough
- bl sync / bl prime walkthrough
- tracker adopt / disown verbs
- Federation: shadow config, center remote.yaml, one-hop
- Pre/post hook rollback semantics
- States as extensible config
- ID generation scheme
- bl doctor: drift checks, stale worktree cleanup
- Migration path from current balls
